320.reason:
[The condition `ch != EOF` is evaluated after `ch == '
'`, but if `ch` is EOF, `ch == '
'` is false, so the loop never exits on EOF. Also, EOF is an `int`, not a `char`, so comparing it with `'
'` (a `char`) can cause issues due to sign extension. This leads to infinite loop or undefined behavior when encountering EOF in single-line comment.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition `ch != EOF` is evaluated after `ch == '\n'`, but if `ch` is EOF, `ch == '\n'` is false and the loop continues indefinitely. Also, EOF is not handled inside the loop, leading to infinite loop on unterminated line comment at EOF.]
